steps.title: Condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has infinitely many solutions when the two equations represent the same line, meaning one equation is a constant multiple of the other.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $5x+3y=15$ and $5x+15y=75$: The coefficient ratios do not match, so these are distinct lines. ✗
  • Choice B — $x+3y=15$ and $x+15y=75$: The coefficient ratios do not match, so these are distinct lines. ✗
  • Choice C — $x+3y=15$ and $5x+15y=75$: Multiplying the first equation by 5 gives $5x+15y=75$, exactly the second equation. Same line, infinitely many solutions. ✓
  • Choice D — $5x+3y=15$ and $x+15y=75$: The coefficient ratios do not match, so these are distinct lines. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
